$comment: c64 (D-CSUBSET-TERNARY-ARRAY-DECAY, C 6.3.2.1p3 + 6.5.15): a conditional with two STRING-LITERAL (array) arms decays EACH arm to a pointer-to-element, so the result is the common POINTER type, never an aggregate. Frontier sqlite3.c:82807 `isFreeList ? "size" : "overflow list length"` fired H0009 (D-CSUBSET-AGGREGATE-VALUED-CONTROL-EXPR): combineTernary (BOTH tiers) computed the result via commonArithType(thenT,elseT) -> Invalid for two arrays, the null-ptr/fn arms missed, and the fallback returned the FIRST arm's `Array<char,5>` -> the ternary typed as an aggregate. An aggregate-valued conditional has no aggregate-width SSA value (a phi-of-aggregate cannot pack bit-fields), so hir_to_mir's ternaryAggregateGuard fired H0009. c64 adds a C 6.3.2.1p3 array-decay arm to both combineTernary lambdas (semantic_analyzer.cpp + cst_to_hir.cpp): when both arms decay to the SAME pointer type (interner.operands(arr)[0] -> interner.pointer(elem), matching element type), the result is that pointer; at the HIR tier `common` then drives the coerce() Array->Ptr decay arm (the sameElem Cast) on each arm, materializing a GlobalAddr to each rodata string. pick(1)='size' read through the decayed `char*` (s1[0..4]); pick(0)='overflow list length' (s0[0],s0[8]=' ',s0[9]='l'); two inline forms exercise both arm orders -> 8 checks pass -> 42. PRESERVE: genuine struct/union ternary arms do NOT decay (no array-to-pointer rule) -> they stay aggregates and reach the by-address ternary lowering (aggregate_ternary_rvalue stays green). Conservative: incompatible-element conditionals stay on the existing fallback. RED-ON-DISABLE: revert the combineTernary array-decay arms -> the conditional types `char[5]`/aggregate -> H0009 at HIR lowering (the exact sqlite error).
